meaning: n.倾覆,破灭,革命vt.推翻,颠倒vi.翻倒n.翻转,毁灭
examples: Recent evidence of subtle interactions between earthquakes may overturn this assumption, however.
They overturn rocks to find them and consuming as many as 40,000 apiece in a single day.
The state already has a "trigger law" on the books, which would ban most abortions, if the Supreme Court overturn Roe v Wade.
In April 2010, the Supreme Court upheld a 2007 federal court move to overturn his pardon.
Despite the merits of the issue, I hate to see the Supreme Court overturn a referendum.
